threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check Google Maps for bus times instead of just knowing that the 38 comes every 10 minutes. Classic newbie move.
Last Friday on the 333, a guy serenaded the entire bus with a surprising opera piece—made the ride from Brixton to Clapham quite the show.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard someone argue passionately in three languages while waiting for the bus at Hermannplatz. That’s when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a slice of pizza at 3 AM and see a spontaneous street party with live DJs and artists turning the pavement into a dance floor.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's from here if they can name the exact bakery where all the postcard pigeons flutter around at noon.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week I heard a saxophonist playing 'Take Five' while the Muni was delayed for a dog on the tracks. Only in SF do we get a concert with our wait.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the parks and cafes, but the real vibe is in the small corners where you can hear families arguing in Catalan over Sunday lunch.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll spot them staring bewildered at the tram schedule, oblivious to the fact that it only runs every 15 minutes on weekends.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still carry your umbrella on sunny days, thinking it’ll rain any minute. Welcome to London, mate; it’s all part of the charm.
Caught the N68 last Saturday, and a bloke serenaded the whole bus with some Elvis. Even the driver chuckled. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing Fußball on the street, dodging traffic like it was part of the game. That's when it clicked.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you grab a currywurst after 3 AM while discussing the latest art installations between kebab shops. It's a unique post-party vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their rhythm: they know the Muni schedule better than the app and will still complain about it at the bus stop.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus that stopped mid-route because a pigeon decided it was its personal stage. Driver just sighed and let the show go on.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'punto' means score in a game, but here in Gràcia, it's just a convenient point to meet, never an end of an argument.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them by their confusion over the 'Tobo' (the tiny kiosks) — locals know it's for quick snacks, not souvenirs.
